__schema0.title: Reference Object
__schema0.description: A reference to another component in the specification.

examples.description: A user is a type of person.
A pet is a type of animal.
A pet is a type of animal.

__schema1.title: Reference
__schema1.description: A reference to another component in the specification.

__schema5.title: summary
__schema5.description: A short summary which by default SHOULD override that of the referenced component.

__schema6.title: description
__schema6.description: A description which by default SHOULD override that of the referenced component.

__schema9.title: name
__schema9.description: The name of the parameter. Case-sensitive.

__schema10.title: in
__schema10.description: Location of the parameter. 'path' parameters must have required=true.

__schema11.title: description
__schema11.description: A description of the parameter. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema16.description: For query parameters only. Allows empty values (?param=).
__schema16.title: allowEmptyValue

__schema18.title: style
__schema18.description: Serialization style. Defaults vary by 'in': query/cookie='form', path/header='simple'.

__schema19.title: explode
__schema19.description: For arrays/objects. When true, each value gets its own parameter.

__schema20.description: For query parameters. When true, allows reserved characters without encoding.
__schema20.title: allowReserved

__schema23.title: Schema Object
__schema23.description: A Schema Object defines the shape of a JSON value.

__schema24.title: Type Array Schema
__schema24.description: OpenAPI 3.1 (JSON Schema) allows type to be an array of primitive types.

__schema25.title: title
__schema25.description: A title for the schema, used for documentation.

__schema26.title: description
__schema26.description: A description of the schema. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema27.title: default
__schema27.description: The default value for this schema.

__schema28.title: example
__schema28.description: An example value for this schema.

__schema31.title: enum
__schema31.description: An array of valid values for this schema.

__schema33.title: const
__schema33.description: A constant value that this schema must match.

__schema35.title: Discriminator
__schema35.description: When request bodies or response payloads may be one of a number of different schemas, a discriminator object can be used to aid in serialization, deserialization, and validation.

__schema36.title: propertyName
__schema36.description: The name of the property in the payload that will hold the discriminator value.

__schema37.title: mapping
__schema37.description: An object to hold mappings between payload values and schema names or references.

__schema42.title: XML
__schema42.description: A metadata object that allows for more fine-tuned XML model definitions.

__schema43.title: name
__schema43.description: Replaces the name of the element/attribute used for the described schema property.

__schema44.title: namespace
__schema44.description: The URI of the namespace definition.

__schema45.title: prefix
__schema45.description: The prefix to be used for the name.

__schema46.description: Declares whether the property definition translates to an attribute instead of an element.
__schema46.title: attribute

__schema48.description: May be used only for an array definition. Signifies whether the array is wrapped or not.
__schema48.title: wrapped

__schema52.title: ExternalDocumentation
__schema52.description: Allows referencing an external resource for extended documentation.

__schema53.title: description
__schema53.description: A short description of the target documentation.

__schema54.title: url
__schema54.description: The URL for the target documentation.

__schema56.title: readOnly
__schema56.description: When true, the property is only returned in responses, not accepted in requests.

__schema57.title: writeOnly
__schema57.description: When true, the property is only accepted in requests, not returned in responses.

__schema58.title: deprecated
__schema58.description: When true, indicates this schema is deprecated and should be avoided.

__schema62.title: String Object
__schema62.description: A String Object defines the shape of a string value.

__schema64.title: format
__schema64.description: Semantic format hint for string values. Common formats: date-time, email, uuid, uri, hostname, ipv4, byte, binary.

__schema65.title: Pattern
__schema65.description: A regular expression pattern the string must match.

__schema70.title: Number Object
__schema70.description: A Number Object defines the shape of a number value.

__schema72.title: format
__schema72.description: Number precision format. Use 'float' for 32-bit single-precision or 'double' (default) for 64-bit double-precision IEEE 754.

__schema73.title: multipleOf
__schema73.description: Require the number to be a multiple of this value.

__schema75.title: minimum
__schema75.description: Inclusive lower bound for the number.

__schema77.title: maximum
__schema77.description: Inclusive upper bound for the number.

__schema79.title: exclusiveMinimum
__schema79.description: Exclusive lower bound (instance must be > this value).

__schema81.title: exclusiveMaximum
__schema81.description: Exclusive upper bound (instance must be < this value).

__schema83.title: Integer Object
__schema83.description: An Integer Object defines the shape of an integer value.

__schema85.title: format
__schema85.description: Integer size format. Use 'int32' for signed 32-bit (-2B to 2B) or 'int64' for signed 64-bit integers.

__schema86.title: multipleOf
__schema86.description: Require the integer to be a multiple of this value.

__schema88.title: minimum
__schema88.description: Inclusive lower bound for the integer.

__schema90.title: maximum
__schema90.description: Inclusive upper bound for the integer.

__schema92.title: exclusiveMinimum
__schema92.description: Exclusive lower bound (instance must be > this value).

__schema94.title: exclusiveMaximum
__schema94.description: Exclusive upper bound (instance must be < this value).

__schema96.title: Boolean Object
__schema96.description: A Boolean Object defines the shape of a boolean value.

__schema98.title: Null Object
__schema98.description: A Null Object defines the shape of a null value.

__schema100.title: Array Object
__schema100.description: An Array Object defines the shape of an array value.

__schema104.title: prefixItems
__schema104.description: Tuple validation: schemas for items by index (array position).

__schema106.title: contains
__schema106.description: Require the array to contain at least one item matching this subschema.

__schema118.title: Object Object
__schema118.description: An Object Object defines the shape of an object value.

__schema142.title: Composition Schema
__schema142.description: A schema using composition keywords (allOf, anyOf, oneOf, not, if/then/else) without a type constraint.

__schema143.title: allOf
__schema143.description: Require the instance to validate against all subschemas.

__schema145.title: anyOf
__schema145.description: Require the instance to validate against at least one subschema.

__schema147.title: oneOf
__schema147.description: Require the instance to validate against exactly one subschema.

__schema149.title: not
__schema149.description: Require the instance to NOT validate against the given subschema.

__schema151.title: if
__schema151.description: Conditional validation: if the instance matches this schema, apply then; otherwise apply else.

__schema155.title: else
__schema155.description: Subschema applied when if does not match.

__schema158.title: examples
__schema158.description: Multiple named examples. Mutually exclusive with 'example'.

__schema165.title: externalValue
__schema165.description: A URL that points to the literal example.

__schema167.title: content
__schema167.description: For complex parameters. Mutually exclusive with 'schema'.

__schema169.title: MediaType
__schema169.description: Each Media Type Object provides schema and examples for the media type identified by its key.

__schema177.title: Encoding
__schema177.description: A single encoding definition applied to a single schema property.

__schema178.title: contentType
__schema178.description: The Content-Type for encoding a specific property.

__schema181.title: Header
__schema181.description: The Header Object follows the structure of the Parameter Object.

__schema182.title: Header
__schema182.description: The Header Object follows the structure of the Parameter Object.

__schema183.title: description
__schema183.description: A brief description of the parameter.

__schema184.description: Determines whether this parameter is mandatory.
__schema184.title: required

__schema186.description: Specifies that a parameter is deprecated.
__schema186.title: deprecated

__schema188.description: Sets the ability to pass empty-valued parameters.
__schema188.title: allowEmptyValue

__schema190.title: style
__schema190.description: Describes how the parameter value will be serialized.
